regulatory.note: You must be on the NMC midwives part of the register to practise as a midwife in the UK.

routes.title: BSc (Hons) Midwifery
routes.summary: Three-year full-time undergraduate degree approved by the NMC, with clinical placements throughout.
routes.typicalCostLabel: Tuition-fee loan available; NHS Learning Support Fund non-repayable grant of at least £5,000 per academic year for eligible students in England

routes.title: MSc pre-registration Midwifery (shortened)
routes.summary: Postgraduate pre-registration programme for graduates in a relevant health or science subject, typically two years full time.
routes.typicalCostLabel: Postgraduate funding routes; NHS Learning Support Fund grant of at least £5,000 per academic year for eligible students in England

routes.title: Registered adult nurse converting to midwifery
routes.summary: Shortened midwifery programme for registered adult nurses on the NMC register.
routes.typicalDurationLabel: Around 18 months to two years, depending on provider
routes.typicalCostLabel: Funding varies by provider; check NMC-approved conversion programmes

requirements.label: GCSEs in maths, English and a science
requirements.description: Most NMC-approved BSc Midwifery programmes require GCSE grade 4/C or above in maths, English and a science subject. Individual universities set the specific requirement.
requirements.verifiedBy: The university's admissions team, on the specific course page

requirements.label: Level 3 qualification (A-levels, T Level or Access to HE)
requirements.description: Universities typically require A-levels including a science, an appropriate T Level, or an Access to Higher Education Diploma (Nursing/Midwifery/Health).
requirements.verifiedBy: The specific university course page on UCAS

requirements.label: First degree in a relevant subject
requirements.description: MSc pre-registration programmes typically require a 2:1 or above in a health or science subject. The exact list varies by provider.
requirements.verifiedBy: The provider's postgraduate midwifery course page

requirements.label: NMC-registered adult nurse
requirements.description: You must already be on the NMC register as an adult nurse to enter a shortened conversion programme.
requirements.verifiedBy: Your entry on the NMC register

requirements.label: Occupational health clearance
requirements.description: All routes require occupational health clearance covering vaccinations and fitness to practise.
requirements.verifiedBy: The provider's occupational health service after conditional offer

requirements.label: Enhanced DBS check
requirements.description: You will need an enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) check because the role involves close contact with vulnerable people.

questionRefs.label: Do you have GCSE grade 4/C or above in maths, English and a science?
Do you have a Level 3 qualification (A-levels, T Level or Access to HE)?
Do you have a first degree in a relevant health or science subject?
Are you currently on the NMC register as an adult nurse?
Do you need income while studying?
Can you commit to full-time clinical placements including nights and weekends?
Are there any factors that might affect an enhanced DBS check?
Any current health conditions that could affect fitness to practise?

then.reason: Most BSc Midwifery courses require GCSE 4/C in maths, English and a science. You will typically need to gain these before applying.

then.check: Confirm the specific GCSE grades each shortlisted university will accept, and whether Functional Skills Level 2 is accepted in place of any missing GCSE.
Ask the university's admissions team which international qualifications they accept in place of GCSEs and whether IELTS or equivalent English-language evidence is required.

then.reason: BSc Midwifery requires a Level 3 qualification such as A-levels, a relevant T Level, or an Access to Higher Education Diploma (Nursing/Midwifery/Health).

then.check: Confirm with the university whether your A-level combination is accepted without a science subject; several courses will require Biology or an Access to HE Diploma in place of it.

then.reason: The MSc pre-registration route requires a completed degree in a relevant health or science subject at 2:1 or above.

then.check: Check with each MSc provider whether your degree subject is accepted, or whether a top-up module in life sciences is needed first.

then.reason: The shortened nurse-to-midwife route is only open to nurses already on the NMC adult-nursing part of the register.

then.concern: Since you are already on the NMC adult-nursing register, the three-year BSc route would take longer than a shortened conversion programme.
The MSc pre-registration route is designed for graduates who are not already registered nurses; the shortened conversion route is typically faster in your case.

Full-time placements (including nights and weekends) make paid work alongside the course difficult. The NHS Learning Support Fund provides a non-repayable grant of at least £5,000 per academic year for eligible students in England.
Full-time placements make paid work alongside the course difficult. The NHS Learning Support Fund grant is also available on eligible pre-registration MSc programmes in England.

then.reason: NMC-approved programmes require full-time clinical placements including night and weekend shifts. This is a regulator requirement, not a provider choice.
Placement patterns are set by the NMC and cannot be reduced below full time.
Placement patterns are set by the NMC and cannot be reduced below full time.

then.check: Speak with the university's admissions team and, if appropriate, an organisation such as Unlock about how your specific circumstances would be considered under the NMC's fitness-to-practise process.
Speak with the provider's admissions team about how your specific circumstances would be considered under the NMC's fitness-to-practise process.
Occupational health clearance is required for every route. Speak to the provider's occupational health service after a conditional offer about your specific circumstances.

then.text: As an NMC-registered adult nurse, the shortened conversion route is likely to be the fastest path onto the midwives part of the register.

actionTemplates.title: Look into how to gain the required GCSEs
actionTemplates.description: Contact a local further education college about GCSE resit options in maths, English and a science, or a Functional Skills Level 2 route. Ask each shortlisted midwifery provider which they accept.
actionTemplates.effortLabel: One to two hours of research

actionTemplates.title: Check the English-language evidence requirement
actionTemplates.description: Ask two universities that offer midwifery whether they accept your international qualifications, and what IELTS score (or equivalent) they require.

actionTemplates.title: Explore Access to Higher Education Diplomas in Nursing/Midwifery
actionTemplates.description: Look up Access to HE Diplomas (Nursing, Midwifery or Health) at local FE colleges. These are widely accepted in place of A-levels for midwifery programmes.

actionTemplates.title: Check NHS Learning Support Fund eligibility
actionTemplates.description: Read the NHS Business Services Authority page on the Learning Support Fund and check whether your circumstances would qualify you for the training grant, parental support and specialist subject payment.

testProfiles.label: School leaver with science A-levels, no dependants, can commit to placements
School leaver with A-levels but no science subject
Adult nurse on the NMC register wanting to convert
Has maths and English but not science GCSE
Applicant with historic factors that may affect DBS
Applicant with current health condition to check
